body:
## Goal
Provide a dependency-free Node.js HTTP server that answers GET / with a Hello, World! HTML response, launchable via npm start.

## Spec
Server uses only the Node.js built-in `http` module (ESM, no external web framework). It listens on `process.env.PORT` when set, defaulting to 3000. A GET request to path `/` returns HTTP 200 with header `Content-Type: text/html` and a body containing the exact substring `Hello, World!`. package.json declares `"type": "module"` and a `"start"` script that launches the single source file with node. Implementation footprint is exactly one source file plus package.json.

## Changes
- `server.js` — creates
- `package.json` — refactors-existing

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: package.json sets "type": "module" and defines a "start" script that runs the single source file with node.
- [ ] AC-2: Starting the server with no PORT set makes it listen on port 3000; setting PORT overrides that port.
- [ ] AC-3: A GET request to / returns HTTP status 200.
- [ ] AC-4: The response to GET / carries a Content-Type header of text/html.
- [ ] AC-5: The response body for GET / contains the exact text "Hello, World!".
- [ ] AC-6: The implementation consists of exactly one source file plus package.json, with no external web-framework dependency.

## Verify
- node --check server.js (validate)
- PORT=3100 node server.js & then curl -si http://localhost:3100/ shows HTTP/1.1 200, Content-Type: text/html, and body containing Hello, World! (e2e)
- node server.js & then curl -si http://localhost:3000/ confirms the default port 3000 (e2e)

## Non-Goals
- Routing beyond GET /
- External web frameworks or dependencies
- TLS/HTTPS, logging, or graceful-shutdown handling

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"Establish a minimal, framework-free HTTP entrypoint that serves the Hello, World! page."} -->

title: Minimal ESM HTTP server returning "Hello, World!" on GET /
